title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with fervor, showcasing new and old songs to an enthusiastic audience.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist energized the crowd with his charismatic presence, blending self-aggrandizing humor with engaging performances.
Australian band The Chats opened, offering a garage-punk sound, setting the stage for The Hives' high-energy show.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
McHugh, a cancer survivor, returned to running after chemotherapy and surgeries for Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosed in 2023.
This will be McHugh's third Boston Marathon and fourth marathon overall, inspired by her late friend Susan Hurley.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senator Ed Markey questions Meta's plans for facial recognition in smart glasses, citing privacy concerns.
Markey, with Senators Ron Wyden and Jeff Merkley, demands Meta explain data handling by April 6.
The inquiry precedes nationwide protests, including "No Kings" rallies in Massachusetts on March 28.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council delayed a vote on a $2.65M plan to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals.
Mayor Thomas Koch requested council approval to fund PFAS-free turnout gear to reduce health risks for firefighters.
Councilors cited cost and durability concerns; decision to be reconsidered in coming weeks.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose converting Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address $57 million in needed repairs.
Mayor Cathleen DeSimone suggests the transition to reduce costs while maintaining public access with potential free or reduced admission.
Zoo veterinarian Bekah Weatherington criticizes the plan, calling it poorly thought-out and a step towards closing the zoo.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a microwedding at Bow Market due to a blizzard disrupting their initial plans.
The FX series 'Love Story' revisits John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's romance, highlighting 1990s communication styles.
Meredith Goldstein questions the quick adoption of the term 'partner' in modern relationships in her Love Letters newsletter.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, MA, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback lost his father to glioblastoma in 2020 and survived testicular cancer diagnosed in 2021.
Despite a recent serious blood infection, Averback is on track to complete the Boston Marathon.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard works out with WR A.J. Brown amid trade interest from New England.
Eagles seek first-round pick for A.J. Brown, rumored as Patriots trade target since last season.
Photos of Byard and Brown together heighten speculation of a potential Patriots-Eagles trade deal.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points, sparking speculation he will turn pro soon.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney hinted Hagens may join Boston for their final regular-season games, bolstering their playoff push.
Hagens, the seventh pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, could enhance Boston's offense, potentially playing left wing or third line.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in 'Is This Thing On?' available on Disney+, a comedy-drama centered on stand-up comedy.
Chuck Norris' 1983 film 'Lone Wolf McQuade' is streaming on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' featuring Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan is now streaming on Netflix.
